headline: Open-weights model releases drive a coding and multimodal push while agent reliability and prompt-injection defense break out.
body: Two open-weights models anchored the week: MiniMax M3 targets coding agents and tool use with a million-token context window and Anthropic and OpenAI API compatibility, and GLM 5.2 shipped multimodal capabilities spanning 3D generation, ray tracing, and music composition. On the technique side, quantization, scheduling, and advantage weighting all rotated up and broke out, alongside new entrants in agent skills, parameter sharing, adaptive computation, iterative refinement, and prompt-injection defense. Agent reliability surfaced as a concrete failure mode, with one talk showing web-accessing agents claiming searches they never completed because of blocking and CAPTCHAs. Tooling movers including integrations, AI features, ray tracing, and game physics reinforce the multimodal and agent-workflow direction.
